domain: Glasswing butterfly nano-pillar anti-reflection (random sub-wavelength pillars produce gradient refractive index from air to chitin; omnidirectional anti-reflection)
specific_mechanism: Random-amplitude, sub-wavelength surface texture producing a smooth gradient effective refractive index, eliminating boundary reflection across visible spectrum and incidence angles.
llm_application: GLASS-ATTN: pre-attention input-projection stack of K sub-layers whose weight scales form a monotonically tapered random-amplitude profile (largest at base/input, smallest at tip/attention-space), enforcing a gradient embedding-norm transition from raw input embeddings to attention input. Targets suppression of activation 'reflection' (gradient discontinuity, spurious activation echo) at the input/attention boundary, analogous to optical anti-reflection.
novelty_claim: Gated attention (2505.06708) gates information; Signal Embedding Energy (2601.07331) quantifies noise; gradient-trained embedding analyses (2505.17282) characterize but don't prescribe tapered amplitude scaffolds. None of these impose a random-amplitude gradient-norm taper across K stacked input projections to eliminate input-boundary reflection.
